briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for July 7, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Accelerating Domestic and International Impacts of Climate Change
sections.content: Recently, the Korean Peninsula has shown clear signs of climate change, with alternating heavy monsoon rains and sunshine, longer summers, and more frequent heatwaves [1, 2]. This has led to a widespread perception that 'seasons have disappeared' [3]. Internationally, reports continue to emerge that spring is arriving earlier than usual due to climate change, causing confusion and threats to migratory birds [30, 34, 38, 46]. Furthermore, research indicates that the heatwaves during the World Cup would have been virtually impossible without climate change [39, 45]. Extreme weather events are becoming more frequent, with wildfires raging across Southern Europe, forcing thousands to evacuate [49]. Climate change is exerting wide-ranging impacts, threatening historical sites due to rising sea levels [29], affecting the survival of the Pilbara barking gecko [37], and disrupting raw material markets in conjunction with the El Niño phenomenon [36]. Notably, an Australian study revealed that extreme heat doubles the risk of mental health-related hospitalizations among young people, underscoring the severe societal impact of the climate crisis [50].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts are underway to address climate change. Hongseong County conducted landslide drills in response to climate change, strengthening residents' evacuation capabilities [4]. Gangneung Girls' High School and Catholic Kwandong University launched street campaigns to raise awareness about the climate crisis [5]. Daejeon Daedeok-gu operates carbon neutrality experience programs utilizing air-purifying plants, while various organizations and companies, including iM Financial Group, Cheonan Carbon Neutrality Support Center, and Volunteer Center, are expanding ESG management and social contribution activities through carbon neutrality campaigns and agreements [7, 8, 9, 10, 13]. Special commendations were awarded to public officials who achieved electric vehicle dissemination targets [11], and Naju City is fostering key rural leaders in carbon neutrality and agricultural safety [12]. Efforts to expand renewable energy are also active: Yesan County is promoting a housing support project to assist 50 households with the installation of renewable energy facilities [15, 17, 20, 21, 22]. The Yeongam County Mayor expressed a commitment to transform the region's future through the creation of RE100 industrial complexes and the utilization of renewable energy [23]. POSCO significantly increased its renewable energy usage through solar and small hydropower generation [18], and the three major domestic power companies are focusing on securing direct current transmission and distribution technologies in line with renewable energy expansion [14]. Furthermore, Infineon opened a 'Smart Power Fab' for semiconductor production for AI data centers, electric vehicles, and renewable energy [24]. Ferrari also incorporated a 100% renewable energy management concept [25], and Hanwha Q CELLS secured a solar power plant project from Meta, indicating active private sector investment and technological development [26]. The Korea New & Renewable Energy Association is also committed to nurturing future talent by promoting a plan to cultivate top-tier research personnel [16].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Response and Research Trends
sections.content: The international community recognizes the severity of climate change and is responding through various approaches. China launched its 16th Arctic expedition focused on climate change, enhancing scientific understanding [41], and the second UVic satellite launch is also expected to contribute to advancements in climate change research [43]. In Canada's Atlantic region, interest in nature-based solutions is growing, with explorations into how nature can combat climate change [42]. In the policy and legal spheres, the International Court of Justice (ICJ) issued advisory opinions on climate change and cyber operations, sparking international legal discussions [32]. Climate change has also emerged as a key voting issue in midterm elections, drawing significant political attention [33]. Furthermore, academia and institutions are strengthening their climate response capabilities, with Klein appointed as interim Vice-Provost for Climate and Sustainability Programs [35]. The news that even Europe, a leader in net-zero initiatives, is facing climate change serves as a reminder that the climate crisis is a global issue [47].

sections.title: ⚠️ Significant Challenges in Responding to the Climate Crisis
sections.content: Globally, the failure to reduce carbon emissions and warnings that we may be approaching a 'point of no return' emphasize the urgency of responding to the climate crisis [44]. Domestically, the institutional foundation remains inadequate, with only 13 dedicated carbon neutrality organizations nationwide, and even a lack of climate crisis response committees in Daegu's Buk-gu and Suseong-gu [6]. A new challenge highlighted is the immense energy consumption of Artificial Intelligence (AI), presenting a new concern for sustainability leaders [48]. Furthermore, criticism that a hedge fund operated by a co-owner of GB News nearly tripled its investments in fossil fuel companies in Q1 2026 demonstrates the existence of capital flows that run counter to climate crisis response efforts [51]. At the corporate level, specific efforts to achieve targets are further required, as exemplified by Coway's renewable energy usage being only 2.9%, which remains a task for advancing ESG [19].

briefing.closing: As we have reviewed in today's briefing, climate change is already having severe impacts across our lives and ecosystems, and significant challenges persist alongside domestic and international efforts to address it. This concludes the climate briefing for July 7, 2026.

articles.description: Is the Era of AC Fading with the Expansion of Renewable Energy and Data Centers? Three Major Power Companies Vying for DC Transmission and Distribution Technology - Chosunbiz

summary.phenomenon: With the growing expansion of renewable energy and data centers, Korea's three major power companies are intensely focused on securing direct current (DC) transmission and distribution technology, moving away from the traditional alternating current (AC) methods.
summary.cause: Renewable energy generation typically produces direct current (DC), and data centers benefit from DC use for enhanced power efficiency. Consequently, their expansion is driving an increased need for DC transmission and distribution technology.
summary.outlook: It is anticipated that once the three power companies secure DC transmission and distribution technology, it will enable more efficient power supply to renewable energy sources and data centers.
